title: Manchild
lyrics:
[Intro]
Oh boy
  > [#1, 30 votes] According to Sabrina for the July edition of Rolling Stone, this phrase serves as a re-introduction into the narrative attempted to be presented by Carpenter after her short but sweet musical hiatus:...

[Verse 1]
You said your phone was broken, just forgot to charge it
  > [#2, 62 votes] This could be a reference to Sabrina’s track “Coincidence”, where she sings: What a surprise, your phone just died In “Manchild”, the phone shows how dumb he is, whereas in “Coincidence”, he’s making...
Whole outfit you're wearing, God, I hope it's ironic
  > [#3, 32 votes] Sabrina uses biting sarcasm here to call out the subject’s fashion choices, implying that the outfit is so questionable or mismatched that she hopes it’s being worn ironically, as a joke or...
Did you just say you're finished? Didn't know we started
  > [#4, 75 votes] This line is reminiscent of the first track on the deluxe edition of Short n’ Sweet, “15 Minutes,” on which Carpenter sings: I can do a lot with fifteen minutes Only gonna take two to make you finish...
It's all just so familiar, baby, what do you call it?
  > [#5, 17 votes] She takes a fairly braggadocious angle on this first verse, talking to a boy, likely suspicious he was cheating on her. She is saying how she has been in countless relationships, and even if this guy...

[Pre-Chorus]
Stupid
Or is it slow?
Maybe it's useless?
But there's a cuter word for it, I know

[Chorus]
Man-child
  > [#6, 52 votes] Sabrina calls this man a “manchild,” a phrase that is used to refer to an immature man who refuses to grow up or take responsibility. Here, she asks him why he constantly asks for emotional support...
Why you always come a-running to me?
Fuck my life
Won't you let an innocent woman be?
Never heard of self-care
  > [#7, 21 votes] It is common that in children’s minds there is not much self-care because they have not matured that part of their awareness, this man projects that energy.
Half your brain just ain't there
  > [#8, 33 votes] The lyric “Half your brain just ain’t there” is a comedic takedown of men who look grown but act like they skipped a few stages of emotional development. In an Instagram caption about the project,...
Man-child
Why you always come a-running, taking all my loving from me?

[Verse 2]
Why so sexy if so dumb?
  > [#9, 40 votes] With this line, Sabrina possibly alludes to an unreleased track titled “Why So Sexy?” It also alludes to her track, “Dumb & Poetic”, in which she sung, You’re so dumb and poetic, It’s just what I...
And how survive the Earth so long?
  > [#10, 31 votes] Likely referencing the concept of natural selection, Sabrina is questioning how men as a group have survived over the course of human evolution, since they frequently can’t seem to do anything for...
If I'm not there, it won't get done
  > [#11, 12 votes] At this point in the song, Sabrina has conveyed her annoyance at always having to pick up after this man’s mistakes. Sabrina has expectations that he can handle doing what has to get done, but her...
I choose to blame your mom
  > [#12, 49 votes] Sabrina blames her partner’s upbringing from his mother for his inability to handle his responsibilities on his own, inferring that he was coddled by his mother and she did not give him independence....

[Chorus]
Man-child
  > [#6, see above]
Why you always come a-running to me?
Fuck my life
Won't you let an innocent woman be?
Never heard of self-care
  > [#7, see above]
Half your brain just ain't there
  > [#8, see above]
Man-child
Why you always come a-running, taking all my loving from me?

[Bridge]
Oh, I like my boys playing hard to get
Oh, I like my boys playing hard to get (Play hard to get)
  > [#13, 37 votes] Carpenter is sarcastically commenting on her pattern of choosing emotionally unavailable or immature partners, similar to the line in the track “Good Graces”: Don’t mistake my nice for naive These...
And I like my men all incompetent
And I swear they choose me, I'm not choosing them
And I swear they choose me, I'm not choosing them (Not choosing them)
  > [#14, 29 votes] This lyric was spotted on a billboard before the release of the song.
Amen, hey, men
Amen (Amen), hey, men (Hey, men)
  > [#15, 33 votes] This lyric was first teased at a billboard on interstate-69. In and of itself, this lyric can be interpreted as a message to men in general to stop being so childish. Having said that, “Hey, men” is...
And I like my men all incompetent (Incompetent)

[Chorus]
Man-child
  > [#6, see above]
Why you always come a-running to me? (Always come a-running to me)
Fuck my life
Won't you let an innocent woman be? (Amen)

[Outro]
Oh, I like my boys playing hard to get (Play hard to get)
  > [#13, see above]
And I like my men all incompetent (Incompetent)
And I swear they choose me, I'm not choosing them (Not choosing them)
  > [#14, see above]
Amen (Amen), hey, men (Hey, men, yee-haw)
  > [#15, see above]
